sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: NIO's next EVs use onsemi tech for faster charging, longer range
feed.summary: onsemi has expanded its collaboration with NIO to integrate its EliteSiC enhanced M3e technology into NIO's next-generation 900V EV platforms, moving beyond the current 400V systems. This partnership aims to improve energy efficiency, shorten charging times, and enhance the performance and reliability of NIO's electric vehicles, including models like the ES9 set to debut at the 2026 Beijing Auto Show. The deeper system-level engineering collaboration is expected to accelerate the launch of scalable, high-performance EV platforms.

feed.title: Evercore ISI cuts Booking Holdings stock price target on caution By Investing.com
feed.summary: Evercore ISI has lowered its price target for Booking Holdings (BKNG) to $250 from $6,250, while maintaining an Outperform rating due to caution despite expectations of a modest beat in Q1 earnings. The firm noted that Booking Holdings' Middle East exposure and reliance on Europe and Asia-Pacific for full-year results introduce potential downside variance, leading them to anticipate management will reiterate guidance with incrementally cautious language for the second half of the year. This adjustment follows other recent analyst revisions and developments, including a stock split and strategic acquisitions.

feed.title: Palmer Luckey Says Apple's New CEO John Ternus May Have Built Early VR Gear He Used As A Teen: 'His Only
feed.summary: Palmer Luckey, founder of Oculus, revealed that newly appointed Apple CEO John Ternus might have been the mechanical engineer behind an early virtual reality headset Luckey used as a teenager. Ternus's only non-Apple job was at Virtual Research Systems from 1997 to 2001, where he worked on VR hardware. This connection highlights Ternus's long-standing involvement with hardware engineering, predating his 25-year career at Apple, where he rose to oversee key product developments.

feed.title: BlackRock drops below 3% in Lufthansa shares, holds 4.16% total
feed.summary: BlackRock, Inc. has notified a change in its holdings in Deutsche Lufthansa AG (DLAKY), with its total voting rights decreasing slightly from 4.18% to 4.16% as of April 17, 2026. This stake comprises 2.84% direct shareholdings and 1.32% voting rights through instruments, including lent securities and CFDs. The notification was made under Article 40 (1) WpHG, the German Securities Trading Act, on April 22, 2026.

feed.title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC Is Investigating Forian Inc. (FORA) And Encourages Stockholders to Connect
feed.summary: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC is investigating Forian Inc. (FORA) regarding potential breaches of fiduciary duties by its board of directors. The investigation focuses on the sale of Forian to a consortium led by Max Wygod, where shareholders are set to receive $2.17 per share. The firm is encouraging stockholders who purchased Forian prior to April 2, 2026, and still hold shares, to join the investigation.

feed.title: Gen Digital Inc. (NASDAQ:GEN) Receives Consensus Rating of "Hold" from Analysts
feed.summary: Nine brokerages have assigned Gen Digital Inc. (NASDAQ:GEN) an average "Hold" rating, with a consensus one-year price target of $31.86. The company recently reported quarterly earnings that surpassed analyst expectations and announced a quarterly dividend. Institutional investors actively traded shares, with Vanguard Group Inc. and State Street Corp increasing their holdings.

feed.title: Amcor launches medical packaging plant in Malaysia
feed.summary: Amcor has opened a new healthcare packaging coating plant in Subang Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia, following an investment of over $35m. This facility introduces air-knife coating capability to the region for sterile medical device packaging, enhancing local supply chain resilience and supporting technology adoption. The plant will provide fully integrated manufacturing, dual sourcing capacity, and a base for product trials and large-scale commercial production.

feed.title: Hanwha Ocean and Leidos partner on naval ship design
feed.summary: Hanwha Ocean and Leidos are collaborating to advance next-generation naval ship design, strengthen U.S. shipbuilding capacity, and expand into global defense markets. This partnership combines Hanwha Ocean's shipbuilding scale and Leidos' naval design expertise to deliver scalable and production-ready solutions aligned with U.S. Navy and allied needs. Their focus includes adapting designs to U.S. standards, co-developing new surface combatants, establishing resilient supply chains, and optimizing designs for efficient production and sustainment.

feed.title: Travelers plans to expand homeowners insurance availability across California
feed.summary: Travelers has announced its intention to expand homeowners insurance availability across California by participating in the state's Sustainable Insurance Strategy. This initiative allows insurers to use forward-looking wildfire catastrophe models and reinsurance costs in rates and offers increased discounts for homeowners investing in wildfire mitigation. The move aims to address underserved areas due to wildfire exposure and support access to coverage in the state.
